note: Customers who did not withdraw before the U.S. shutdown deadline moved into a court-mediated access path.
Bittrex filed for bankruptcy and planned to ask the court to reopen accounts for remaining customers.
A U.S. government objection challenged Bittrex's proposal to return customer funds.
A Delaware court authorized withdrawals for customers with qualifying claims.
Withdrawals depended on undisputed and liquidated customer claim status.
Bankruptcy court approval allowed the U.S. operations wind-down plan to proceed.
The bankruptcy court opinion documents customer-claim classes and plan treatment.
